prose:
**Acrolein Fukui Reactivity Calculation**

A Fukui reactivity analysis was performed on acrolein using DFT with the **B3LYP/def2-TZVP** functional-basis pair (tier `standard`, PySCF backend). The geometry was the user-supplied acrolein.xyz file (single-point calculation, not re-optimized). The calculation was run in gas phase at charge 0 and multiplicity 1 for the neutral state; the cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anion (charge −1, multiplicity 2) were computed to determine the Fukui indices via finite-difference charge changes (Mulliken scheme).

**Key Results:**
- **Most electrophilic site:** Terminal carbon (C4, index 3) with f⁺ = 0.6423
- **Most nucleophilic site:** Carbonyl oxygen (O1, index 0) with f⁻ = 0.3939
- **Dual descriptor maximum:** C4 with Δf = 0.5549, strongly predisposed to electrophilic reactivity

The SCF converged successfully for all three electronic states. All charge-conservation checks passed, confirming data integrity. A visualization plot of the Fukui indices has been generated.

**Method:** DFT (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, tier standard), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral); cation and anion at charge ±1, multiplicity 2.</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.6423
